aboutBGDropdown.missionVisionDesc: What we promise and where we are going

nav.mobileDrawerWhereTo: Where would you like to go?

about.heroBody: Since 2019, Bridging Generations has sponsored students across the Chittagong Hill Tracts — covering tuition, books, daily meals, and the materials that keep a child in school instead of pulled into early labor.

missionVision.headline: Education without the barrier of poverty.
missionVision.intro: Two statements that anchor everything we do — what we promise our students today, and the generation we are working toward tomorrow.
missionVision.missionBody: Establishing an enlightened generation through sustainable education bridging — and extending the helping hands to ensure poverty may not be the hindrance to the education of the unprivileged and neglected people.
missionVision.visionBody: Providing scholarships to the poor but talented students. Undertaking different projects and implementing them in the underprivileged group.
missionVision.ctaBody: Every sponsorship keeps one more student in the classroom. Help us carry this work forward.

donationJourney.headline: Five years. Every student, still in school.
donationJourney.intro: Since 2021, Bridging Generations donors have funded tuition, daily meals, books, and materials for children across the Chittagong Hill Tracts. Here is how the programme has grown — year by year, gift by gift.

blog.title: Our blog
blog.description: Posts from board and partner-school staff — what landed, what's next, and what we owe a plain accounting of.

gallery.title: Gallery.
gallery.description: Photographs from partner schools, program visits, and the students we sponsor.

home.missionHeadline: Poverty cannot be the barrier of education
home.missionBody: Establishing an enlightened generation through sustainable education bridging — and extending the helping hands to ensure poverty may not be the hindrance to the education of the unprivileged and neglected people.
home.successHeadline: Students who finished — and what they did next.

homeExtra.ctaHeadline: Join us. Sponsor a child — or apply for a scholarship.
homeExtra.ctaBody: $30 a month covers tuition, books, daily meals, and materials for one student. Students in the Hill Tracts can apply for a scholarship in under 10 minutes.
homeExtra.spotlightBody: Each card represents one sponsored student. First names only; photos render only when a signed, in-scope release is on file — otherwise a neutral placeholder takes its place.
homeExtra.spotlightNote: Want to sponsor a specific student? Open a profile to see how to dedicate your gift.
homeExtra.galleryEmpty: No photographs in this category yet.
homeExtra.testimonialHeadline: Your positive comment is our inspiration.

aboutExtra.einPending: EIN published once the board confirms the filing.
aboutExtra.form990Pending: We publish our IRS Form 990 annually. Links appear here as soon as each year's filing is available.
aboutExtra.stripeReceipts: Stripe processes donations and issues automated receipts at the time of each gift.
aboutExtra.remoteOperations: We operate remotely. Mail reaches us through the email below.
aboutExtra.boardUnpaid: The board is unpaid. 100% of project-designated donations go to program costs.
aboutExtra.boardIntro: The five board members guiding Bridging Generations across continents.
aboutExtra.moderatorIntro: The communicators who keep students, donors, and the board in sync.
aboutExtra.rndIntro: The volunteers researching scholarships, schools, and where to grow next.
aboutExtra.accountingIntro: The people tracking every donated dollar and report.
aboutExtra.coordinatorIntro: On-the-ground coordinators between schools, students, and the board.
aboutExtra.ctaBody: Every sponsorship keeps one more student in the classroom. Help us carry this work into the next year.

mentorsPage.heroBody: Every Bridging Generations student is paired with a volunteer mentor or online teacher. Meet the people who show up every week.
mentorsPage.listHelper: Add or edit mentors in the Keystatic admin under Team members → set Team to Mentor.
mentorsPage.emptyState: No mentors are listed yet. Board admins: add an entry in the Team Members collection with team = Mentor.
mentorsPage.howHeading: Want to mentor or teach online?
mentorsPage.step1Body: Send us a short note via the form on /contact — include your subject, hours per week, and where you live.
mentorsPage.step2Body: The board reviews every applicant. We confirm subject fit and references within three weeks.
mentorsPage.step3Body: Once approved, you're matched with one or more students for at least one term. Weekly check-ins, monthly written report.
mentorsPage.ctaHeadline: Mentor a student through next term.
mentorsPage.ctaBody: One hour a week. Real impact. Apply now and the board will be in touch.

activitiesPage.heroDescription: Short field updates across our partner schools — distribution days, milestones, board visits, and fundraiser news.
activitiesPage.emptyState: No activities match this filter yet.
activitiesPage.ctaHeadline: Support the next update on this list.
activitiesPage.ctaBody: Every activity here is someone's donation at work — a meal served, a scholarship awarded, a delivery made. Start a recurring sponsorship to keep the list going.

testimonialsPage.heroDescription: On what this work has meant to them, in their own words.
testimonialsPage.emptyState: No testimonials in this group yet.
testimonialsPage.ctaBody: Know a parent, teacher, or alum whose story belongs here? Point them our way — we publish with permission.

successStoriesPage.heroDescription: In-depth stories from current students, alumni, and the families who walk the path alongside them. Portraits appear only where a story-release is on file.
successStoriesPage.ctaBody: Sponsorship is how these stories start. $30 a month covers tuition, books, daily meals, and materials for one student — long enough to see a whole arc through.

studentsPageExtra.rulesTitle: What sponsorship requires of our students.
studentsPageExtra.applyHeading: Are you a student? Apply for a scholarship.
studentsPageExtra.applyBody: Create a free student account, share your details, and our board will review your application. Once approved, you'll get a unique Student ID and can sign in to see who is sponsoring you and how much they've given.
studentsPageExtra.ctaHeadline: Your sponsorship puts a name on this page.
studentsPageExtra.ctaBody: Every $30 / month sponsorship covers tuition, books, daily meals, and materials for one student. The next sponsorship pays for the next name on this list.
studentsPageExtra.consentBody: We publish a student's portrait only when their family has signed a written release that names this website specifically. Where that release is missing, pending, or withdrawn, you'll see a neutral illustrated placeholder in its place. Families can revoke consent at any time; the next deploy removes the photo.

projectsPageExtra.heroIntro: Sponsorships keep children in the classroom; projects fund the things the classroom itself needs — meals, books, scholarships, labs, and libraries.
projectsPageExtra.scholarshipsLine: Eligibility, what we cover, and how to apply.
projectsPageExtra.fundedBody: These projects closed with every dollar raised. Huge thanks to the donors who made them happen.
projectsPageExtra.schoolsHeading: The schools where every project is delivered.
projectsPageExtra.schoolsLine: Each partner school has its own overview, student roster, and teacher list.
projectsPageExtra.rulesTitle: How projects, applications, and revocation work.
projectsPageExtra.ctaHeadline: Back a project that moves the needle.
projectsPageExtra.ctaBody: Every gift goes straight to a named line item — not overhead. Start or increase a sponsorship, or direct a gift to a specific project above.

scholarshipsPageExtra.ctaBody: Whether you're an applicant, a guardian, or a sponsor — the next step is the same: send us a note.

donatePageExtra.trustNonprofitBody: Bridging Generations is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit. All gifts are tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law.
donatePageExtra.trustResponseBody: The board responds to gift questions within two business days.
donatePageExtra.trustFallbackBody: Our Stripe donation form is being finalised. In the meantime, write to <email></email> — the board confirms receipt and routes the gift by hand.
donatePageExtra.monthlyHeadline: ${amount}/mo keeps one student in the classroom
donatePageExtra.monthlyLede: Tuition, books, daily meals, and materials for the full school year. The single most leveraged way to give — recurring sponsors are how the program plans staffing.
donatePageExtra.giveOnceBody: Use the form above and choose Give once at checkout — every dollar still routes to program costs.
donatePageExtra.designateBody: Back the meal program, the girls' scholarship fund, or a school-supply delivery.
donatePageExtra.honorBody: Mark a birthday, a teacher, or a family member. Write us and we'll send a card to whomever you name.
donatePageExtra.thankYouHeadline: Your support changes a child's year.
donatePageExtra.shareMessage: I'm sponsoring a student in the Chittagong Hill Tracts via Bridging Generations: https://bridginggenerations.org/donate

contactPageExtra.privacyNotice: We use your email only to reply. No list, no automation.

donorsPageExtra.ctaBody: Your gift funds tuition, daily meals, books, and materials for children across the Chittagong Hill Tracts.

applyPages.mentorHeroBody: One hour a week, real impact. The board reviews every applicant — subject fit and references — within three weeks. Once approved, you're matched with one or more students for at least one term.
applyPages.mentorCtaHeadline: Not a mentor — but want to help?
applyPages.mentorCtaBody: Sponsor a student or back a project. Every $30/month keeps a child in the classroom.
applyPages.scholarshipHeroHeadline: Apply for a Bridging Generations scholarship.
applyPages.scholarshipHeroBody: Open to students enrolled at our partner schools across the Chittagong Hill Tracts. The board reviews every application against published eligibility rules and replies within four weeks.
applyPages.scholarshipCtaHeadline: Not eligible — but want to support?
applyPages.scholarshipCtaBody: If you can't apply but would like to sponsor a student, see how to give.
